title: Nilo (Rodisar)

'''Nilo''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodisar]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rodis]], where he had served as a [[General/Legends|general]] during the planet's seasonal wars. During the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], he was kidnapped and sold to [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattataki]] war barons. As a result, Nilo became a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] in [[the Cauldron]] arena, where he was killed in his very first battle.

==Biography==
A Rodisar from the battlefields of [[Rodis]],<ref name="DB" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]'<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Rodis system/Legends|Rodis system]]</ref> planet that experienced seasonal wars among its primitive inhabitants.<ref name="CE">{{CSWECite|III|105|Rodisar}}</ref> Nilo had once been a general in the so-called [[winter wars]]. Though strong and a capable brawler, in truth, Nilo was a lackluster military leader. He had climbed the corrupt military ranks of Rodis by bribing politicians, while most of his actual military expertise had come from his command staff. Nilo and his command staff were later kidnapped in a [[Zygerrian/Legends|Zygerrian]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] run, and most of his followers were killed trying to protect their general. The slavers sold Nilo to [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattataki]] war barons, impressing them with tales of his supposed military exploits.<ref name="DB" />

[[File:NiloVSGladiator.jpg|280px|left|thumb|General Nilo fighting a gladiator]]
Shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Nilo in this way ended up in [[The Cauldron]] arena.<ref name="CW" /> It was the largest gladiatorial pit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]], filled with violent warriors, where war barons and generals sought out the best combatants who would win their wars.<ref name="DBR">{{DB|location|rattatak|Rattatak}}</ref> Therefore, Nilo, like many other gladiators,<ref name="CW" /> entered a deadly free-for-all battle,<ref name="EGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> but it was a rare gladiator indeed who could survive multiple fights there.<ref name="DBR" /> In his first fight,<ref name="DB" /> Nilo faced an [[Gladiator (Rattatak)|armored gladiator]]. At the same time, [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] [[Mission to Rattatak|entered]] the fray and, with the assistance of fire shells launched by the combat droid [[L8-L9]], killed most of the remaining combatants. Nilo was struck by a fiery blast,<ref name="CW" /> and he did not emerge alive.<ref name="DB" />

==Personality==
Nilo was a [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="DB" /> who belonged to the reptilian species.<ref name="CE" /> Despite having little tactical skill, Nilo climbed the [[Army/Legends|military]] ranks of his [[Rodis|homeworld]] through bribes and pay offs, eventually gaining notoriety for his many «war exploits», which in fact were due more towards his command staff than Nilo himself.<ref name="DB" /> Though Nilo appeared to be a strong and capable brawler, ex-general saw his first real combat in [[the Cauldron]] gladiator pit, from which he did not emerge alive.<ref name="CW" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Pre-Gamorrean.jpg|180px|right|thumb|Nilo was based on an unused Gamorrean Guard concept]]
Nilo<ref name="DB" /> first appeared as an unnamed background character in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> The character was later given a name and short backstory in the personal [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry on the official ''[[Star Wars]]'' website, [[StarWars.com]], in [[2011]].<ref name="DB" />

Nilo's appearance was based on unused concept drawings—done by the artist [[Nilo Rodis-Jamero]]—of the [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards during the production of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="DB" /> His name Nilo, and his species Rodisar, were an homage to the original artist.<ref name="BDB" />
